title: LCL pool in Texas
wikitext:
[]: LCL pool in Texas?             Quote Post by Erizo » Thu Aug 04, 2011 7:04 am [url]http://www.wjla.com/blogs/weather/2011/08/texas-lake-turns-blood-red-apocalypse-begins-photo--12170.html[/url] “The second angel poured out his bowl on the sea, and it became as the blood of a dead man, and every living soul died in the sea," the passage Begley cited reads. "The third angel poured out his bowl upon the rivers and the fountains of water, and they became blood.”

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Aug 04, 2011 9:47 am Did they find Giant Naked Rei's decapitated head yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Trajan » Thu Aug 04, 2011 10:01 am Uh...it looks more brown than anything. Maybe it's the angle they took the picture from but it just looks like mud to me.

[]:             Quote Post by juaxoo » Thu Aug 04, 2011 10:42 am mmyeah they should stop shitting in the lakes

[]:             Quote Post by Deyno » Thu Aug 04, 2011 2:57 pm is not the only place in a desert of Chile there is a red lake http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VNVm2V0V24g and salt parts of Dead Sea are pink

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Thu Aug 04, 2011 3:00 pm The Dead Sea has turned red before. It caused the Egyptians and Yisraelites to believe it an act of the gods. It's caused by a chemical reaction underwater. I'm not entirely sure of the details, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Vegeta 20XX » Thu Aug 04, 2011 3:39 pm BrikHaus wrote: Did they find Giant Naked Rei's decapitated head yet?

[]:             Quote Post by Viking » Thu Aug 04, 2011 4:47 pm I've seen some red "pools" during a school trip ( Elba Island if I remember correctly ) in an old mine. I'm still wondering about the percentage of iron oxide ( Fe 2 O 3 ) in that water... Bye

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Aug 04, 2011 5:02 pm When I saw the topic, I was imagining a pool filled with Tang. Darn.

[]:             Quote Post by Shogo-Kun » Thu Aug 04, 2011 5:11 pm Reichu wrote: When I saw the topic, I was imagining a pool filled with Tang. Darn.
